claim: Enterprises have 10% structured and 90% unstructured data; AI tools must handle both to unlock enterprise value
actionable: When building enterprise AI systems, prioritize handling unstructured data (documents, PowerPoints, PDFs) alongside structured databases
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Hurrey: Scaling Enterprise AI with MCP and A2A

claim: Model Context Protocol standardizes AI-to-tool connections, replacing custom API code with reusable protocol implementations
actionable: Use MCP to standardize how AI systems connect to tools instead of building custom API wrappers for each integration
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Hurrey: Scaling Enterprise AI with MCP and A2A

claim: Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A) enables modular multi-agent systems through standardized agent communication
actionable: Use A2A protocols to build modular agent systems that can communicate and coordinate work without tight coupling
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Hurrey: Scaling Enterprise AI with MCP and A2A

claim: Invoice processing workflows demonstrate how MCP simplifies orchestrating AI across multiple tool integrations
actionable: Use MCP to connect document processing, data extraction, and data integration steps in enterprise workflows
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Scott Hurrey: Scaling Enterprise AI with MCP and A2A
